title: Sony will stop manufacturing PlayStation game discs as of January 2028 and will focus on digital format
summary: Sony will close the PlayStation Store for PS3 and PS Vita and halt production of physical discs for new releases starting in January 2028, ushering in the digital era on its consoles.

title: US lifts restrictions on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models after two-week veto
summary: After two weeks of government veto due to a jailbreak vulnerability, the AI models regain global availability with a new security filter.

title: A vulnerability in Apple's 'Hide My Email' exposes users' real email addresses
summary: The function designed to hide users' real email addresses has a flaw that reveals the true data, compromising privacy.

title: Cinder City is the first game requiring 64 GB of RAM as a recommended requirement
summary: The new indie title sets a new standard in PC hardware requirements, far surpassing the specifications of most current games.

title: The next Xbox Project Helix and PlayStation 6 aim to be fully digital consoles without a disc drive
summary: According to various leaks, both Microsoft and Sony would opt to eliminate the disc drive in their next consoles to promote digital distribution.

title: PC shipments fall 7% in the US during the first quarter of 2026 and an annual drop of 14% is expected
summary: The rise in prices of components such as memory and storage causes a slump in computer sales, especially in low-cost laptops.

title: Meta plans to sell its spare AI computing capacity as a cloud business
summary: Mark Zuckerberg's company seeks to profit from its AI infrastructure investments by renting out excess capacity to third parties.

title: The NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090D reaches 4 GHz for the first time on a Blackwell GPU
summary: GALAX's special HOF OC LAB edition becomes the first GPU of the Blackwell architecture to surpass the 4 GHz barrier through overclocking.

title: Steam Machine gets 2.5% more GPU performance when using dual-channel RAM
summary: Tests show that Valve's console experiences a modest improvement in graphics performance when using RAM in dual-channel configuration.

title: South Korea will invest $520 billion together with Samsung and SK Hynix to lead AI memory
summary: The joint plan of the South Korean government and local tech giants seeks to secure global leadership in the manufacturing of semiconductors for artificial intelligence.

title: The ESA declares private servers for Minecraft and Call of Duty illegal, considering them piracy
summary: The association representing the video game industry warns that unauthorized private servers violate copyright, which halts a legislative initiative to protect online games.

title: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 with lower rates but hides a real increase in price per token
summary: Anthropic's new model is promoted as cheaper but changes in token counting mean a real price increase for developers who use it intensively.

title: Google launches the affordable AI model Nano Banana 2 Lite and the Gemini Omni Flash video generator
summary: Google expands its artificial intelligence ecosystem with more accessible models and new on-demand video generation capabilities.

title: OpenClaw launches native apps for iOS and Android
summary: The open-source platform now offers native apps for mobile devices, facilitating access from anywhere.

title: DeepSeek V4 reduces token cost by up to 80% and NVIDIA optimizes its Blackwell GPUs for Chinese AI
summary: The joint optimization between the Chinese model DeepSeek V4 and NVIDIA's Blackwell GPUs promises a drastic reduction in AI computational cost.

title: US Supreme Court rules that mass collection of location data requires a warrant
summary: The judicial ruling reinforces digital privacy protection by considering that the mass collection of geolocation data without a warrant affects the Fourth Amendment.

title: BYD prepares to surpass Tesla again in electric car sales
summary: The Chinese company continues its global expansion and could temporarily snatch the top spot in electric vehicle sales from Tesla.

title: US evaluates lifting the ban on commercial supersonic flights
summary: The US government analyzes removing historical restrictions that banned supersonic flights over land due to noise, which could revolutionize commercial aviation.

title: Swedish court orders Google to pay $1.5 billion to Klarna for antitrust damages
summary: The conviction for abuse of dominant position in the search and online advertising market marks a milestone in the European antitrust fight against big tech.

title: OpenAI accidentally reveals a new line of 'Pro' models in a genomics article
summary: A scientific article published by OpenAI accidentally includes mentions of a range of more advanced 'Pro' models that the company has not yet officially confirmed.
